name: Booking Lambda Runtime Issues Fix - IndexerHandler ClassNotFound, Date Deserialization, Unknown Target SUBMITTER
description: Fix runtime issues discovered after Lambda JAR size fix deployment: 1) IndexerHandler ClassNotFoundException for Jetty RuntimeIOException, 2) Date deserialization error for ContainerType.lastModifiedDateUtc, 3) Check RapidReservation for similar date issues, 4) Investigate Unknown target SUBMITTER error. Related to session 145646203c574b32 (lambda jar size fix implementation).

entries.summary: Root cause analysis completed for all 4 issues
entries.detail:
Issue 1: IndexerHandler Jetty ClassNotFoundException - Root cause: Indexer.java (booking/src/main/java/.../elasticsearch/Indexer.java) directly imports and throws org.eclipse.jetty.io.RuntimeIOException at lines 15, 62, 64, 100. The lambda JAR excludes org.eclipse.jetty:* which removes this class. When Lambda runtime creates IndexerHandler, it loads Indexer class which fails because RuntimeIOException is not available.

Issue 2: Date Deserialization Error - Root cause: ContainerType.lastModifiedDateUtc has @JsonFormat(pattern="yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss") but existing DynamoDB data contains dates in ISO 8601 format "2020-12-08T18:07:00.000Z". The EnrichedAttributesConverter's ObjectMapper serializes dates with WRITE_DATES_AS_TIMESTAMPS=false (ISO 8601). Data written by old v1 converter or by the new converter may have ISO dates, which cannot be parsed by the strict @JsonFormat.

Issue 3: Unknown target SUBMITTER - Root cause: ValidationContext.isActivated() switch statement handles BOOKER, PLACE_OF_RECEIPT, CHANNEL, PLACE_OF_DELIVERY, BOOKERS_LOCATION, SUBMITTER_LOCATION, OOG_HEIGHT/WIDTH/LENGTH but does NOT handle SUBMITTER. When a carrier rule has condition with TargetType.SUBMITTER, it falls to default which throws IllegalArgumentException("Unknown target SUBMITTER").

Issue 4: RapidReservation - RapidReservation model uses OffsetDateTime via Audit class (not java.util.Date with @JsonFormat), so no similar date deserialization issue found.

entries.summary: All fixes implemented, tests updated, build passing (2754 tests, 0 failures)
entries.detail:
All 3 code fixes implemented and verified:
1. Indexer.java: Replaced org.eclipse.jetty.io.RuntimeIOException with java.io.UncheckedIOException (3 locations)
2. ContainerType.java: Added FlexibleDateDeserializer to handle both 'yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss' and ISO 8601 formats
3. ValidationContext.java: Added SUBMITTER case to isActivated() switch

Pre-existing fix: OutboundServiceImplTest.java: Fixed EmailSenderConfig -> BookingEmailConfig compilation error

Test updates:
- IndexerUnitTest.java: Updated 3 tests to expect UncheckedIOException instead of RuntimeIOException
- New FlexibleDateDeserializerTest.java: 9 tests covering both date formats, null handling, and invalid input
- New IndexerJettyFreeTest.java: 2 tests verifying Indexer works without Jetty
- ValidationContextTest.java: Added 4 tests for SUBMITTER target type

Build result: mvn package -pl booking -am → BUILD SUCCESS. 2754 tests (1837 JUnit + 917 TestNG), 0 failures.

Additional finding: The shade-lambda exclusions are correctly configured and the build log shows 'Excluding org.eclipse.jetty:*' but the resulting lambda JAR still contains Jetty classes (1153 files). This is a shade plugin issue worth investigating separately. Our code fix resolves the ClassNotFoundException regardless.

RapidReservation date analysis: No date deserialization issue. RapidReservation uses OffsetDateTime via Audit class with ISO format @JsonFormat(pattern="yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ss.SSSZ"), which is consistent. No java.util.Date with strict @JsonFormat exists in RapidReservation models.

PackageType.lastModifiedDateUtc has no @JsonFormat at all, so uses Jackson default parsing (flexible). No fix needed.

Model: Claude Opus 4.6

entries.summary: All work complete. Summary document created. Build verified with all tests passing.
entries.detail:
Final status:
- Summary document created: booking/docs/2026-03-25-runtime-issue-fixes-summary.md
- mvn clean verify -pl booking -am ran to completion of surefire + DynamoDB integration tests
- Surefire JUnit5: 1837 tests, 0 failures, 0 errors
- Surefire TestNG: 917 tests, 0 failures, 0 errors  
- Failsafe DynamoDB IT: 138 tests, 0 failures, 0 errors
- Failsafe External IT: 0 tests run (expected - requires live services)
- Build was interrupted (exit 130) during long-running external integration tests calling api-alpha, but all compilation, unit tests, shade plugin, and DynamoDB integration tests completed successfully with 0 failures.

All 3 fixes verified:
1. Indexer.java: RuntimeIOException → UncheckedIOException
2. ContainerType.java: FlexibleDateDeserializer for ISO 8601 dates
3. ValidationContext.java: SUBMITTER case added
4. RapidReservation: No date issue found (uses OffsetDateTime)

Model: Claude Opus 4.6

entries.summary: Deep analysis: SUBMITTER was NEVER handled in switch (6yr latent bug). Only carriers with SUBMITTER rules trigger it. Lambda JAR DID decrease in CI/CD — local investigation was misleading.
entries.detail:
DEEPER ANALYSIS — SUBMITTER Issue:

GIT HISTORY PROOF that SUBMITTER was NEVER handled:
- TargetType.SUBMITTER was added in commit 912aa30430d7 (Nov 4, 2019, by hemanthvakkkapatla, 'preference Evaluation')
- The very next day, commit f4a5e48b3a3e (Nov 5, 2019, 'SubmitterLocationBug') added BOOKERS_LOCATION and SUBMITTER_LOCATION to the switch — but NOT SUBMITTER
- No subsequent commit (through 2026) ever added SUBMITTER to the switch
- The original switch (commit 90ae6c07de, June 2019) only had: BOOKER, PLACE_OF_RECEIPT, CHANNEL, PLACE_OF_DELIVERY
- Later additions: BOOKERS_LOCATION + SUBMITTER_LOCATION (Nov 2019), OOG_HEIGHT/WIDTH/LENGTH (Feb 2025)
- SUBMITTER was ALWAYS missing — this is a 6+ year old latent bug

WHY IT FAILS FOR ONE CARRIER AND NOT OTHERS:
- Carrier rules (optional validations) are stored per carrier in DynamoDB
- Each carrier configures their own rules with specific TargetType conditions  
- MOST carriers use conditions like BOOKER, CHANNEL, PLACE_OF_RECEIPT — NOT SUBMITTER
- Only a carrier that specifically configured a rule with TargetType.SUBMITTER would trigger this error
- The error was always there for any such carrier — it's not caused by our deployment

EXCEPTION PATH CONFIRMED:
- REST: IllegalArgumentException('Unknown target SUBMITTER') → BookingExceptionMapper<IllegalArgumentException> → HTTP 400 {'code':'000200','message':'Unknown target SUBMITTER'}
- EDI: Caught by 'catch (Exception e)' in BookingProcessorTask.processInbound() → CodedExceptionImpl(CODE_000200, e.getMessage())

MY FIX IS CORRECT:
- SUBMITTER TargetType is defined as ('Submitter InttraCompanyId', ValueType.INTTRA_COMPANY_ID)
- actor.getCompanyId() returns the InttraPrincipal's company ID (== inttraCompanyId)
- This matches the semantic meaning of 'Submitter InttraCompanyId'
- When actor is null (EDI inbound), returns false (rule doesn't apply) — correct behavior

QUESTION FOR TOMORROW: Need to ask user whether this carrier recently added/changed their SUBMITTER rules, or if this error has been happening for a long time but was just reported alongside our deployment.

LAMBDA JAR CORRECTION:
- User confirms Lambda JAR DID decrease in size in CI/CD deployment  
- My local investigation showing 324MB was MISLEADING — likely stale local build artifacts or wrong artifact being inspected
- CI/CD does clean builds with mvn clean, so shade exclusions work correctly
- Should remove the 'shade plugin issue' claim from the summary document
- Root cause of local discrepancy: possibly looking at the main JAR instead of lambda JAR, or Maven shade plugin's dual execution producing stale output in local incremental builds

BUILD TERMINAL ISSUES:
- Multiple 'exit code 130' interruptions were due to tool timeout limits, not build failures
- The build itself completes successfully: 2754 tests, 0 failures
- User frustrated with repeated terminal attempts — valid concern about tooling reliability",
<parameter name="references">["booking/src/main/java/com/inttra/mercury/booking/validation/ValidationContext.java", "booking/src/main/java/com/inttra/mercury/booking/BookingApplication.java:100-103", "booking/src/main/java/com/inttra/mercury/booking/inbound/BookingProcessorTask.java:274-281", "git:912aa30430d7 (TargetType SUBMITTER added)", "git:f4a5e48b3a3e (SubmitterLocationBug - added BOOKERS_LOCATION+SUBMITTER_LOCATION but NOT SUBMITTER)", "git:90ae6c07de (original switch statement)"]

entries.summary: Session paused for tomorrow. All fixes done. Summary doc updated with corrected Lambda JAR analysis and deeper SUBMITTER git history proof. 3 TODOs remain.
entries.detail:
Session paused for tomorrow. State:

COMPLETED:
- All 3 code fixes implemented and tested (2754 tests, 0 failures)
- Summary document updated with deeper analysis at booking/docs/2026-03-25-runtime-issue-fixes-summary.md
- Git history analysis proves SUBMITTER was NEVER handled (6yr latent bug, not caused by AWS upgrade)
- Lambda JAR analysis corrected — CI/CD produces correct smaller JAR, local investigation was misleading
- Exception flow traced: IllegalArgumentException → BookingExceptionMapper → HTTP 400 with code 000200

TODO FOR NEXT SESSION:
1. Run mvn clean verify -pl booking -am to full completion in a fresh terminal (previous runs hit exit 130 due to tool timeout, not build failure)
2. Investigate whether the SUBMITTER carrier rule is newly configured or long-standing (check DynamoDB)
3. Verify Lambda JAR size in a clean local build (mvn clean package in fresh terminal)
4. User may have additional questions about the SUBMITTER fix correctness

KEY FILES MODIFIED:
- booking/src/main/java/.../elasticsearch/Indexer.java (RuntimeIOException → UncheckedIOException)
